title: How to remove duplicate lines from files preserving their order
summary: Suppose you have a text file and you need to remove all of its duplicate lines. TL;DR To remove the duplicate lines preserving their order in the file use: awk '!visited[$0]++' your_file > deduplicated_file How it works The script keeps an associative array with indices equal to the unique lines of the file and values equal to their occurrences.
